It is a part of the order Lamiales, which comprises a large group of flowering plants. Within the family Oleaceae, Lupinaster baicalensis is placed in the genus Lupinaster, which is a small group of plants. Further classification shows that it is a species within the genus Lupinaster. The classification of Lupinaster baicalensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the olive family.
uses: Lupinaster baicalensis is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, to treat various health issues.
